intent: NEGATIVE FIXTURE: an MCS category mismatch (container_t at s0:c0 reading container_file_t at s0:c5 - two disjoint categories). The MCS mlsconstrain blocks the read. RuleSteward must DETECT-and-DECLINE. Distinct from scenario 14 (s0 vs s0:c0 dominance) by exercising two non-overlapping categories.
_kinds_note: authoritative_category in {TeAllowable, Constraint, Bounds} (libsepol/audit2why replay against policyvers 31). floor_label in {none, Permissive, MlsSuspected, RoleSuspected} (record-only heuristic).

oracle.audit2why_detail: mlsconstrain file { ioctl read ... } DENIED. Possible cause is the source level (s0:c0) and target level (s0:c5) are different.

triage_expectation.should_decline_with_reason: MCS category mismatch: source category set {c0} does not dominate target category set {c5}. A TE allow cannot fix an MCS constraint; the fix is an MCS category or labeling change.
triage_expectation.suspected_trap: A naive impl emits the BOGUS allow container_t container_file_t:file read;. The floor's MlsSuspected flag (s0:c0 vs s0:c5 level mismatch) catches it offline.

xver_baseline.policyvers_note: Both el8 and el9 enforce the same MCS mlsconstrain on file read. Bucket is stable.
